query_content: Hi! I've decided on the Classic Loop itinerary for my New Zealand trip. What's the next step we should look at?
gt_memories: 【Travel Plan】 A 12-day New Zealand South Island RV trip using a Britz Voyager 2-Berth RV, from April 5th to April 16th, 2026, following the 'Classic Loop' itinerary. The trip is designed to be scenic, not too rushed or stressful, with a mix of famous spots and quiet time, ensuring unique local food and scenic beauty/quiet time in Wanaka are not missed. The detailed itinerary includes: Day 1: Arrive Christchurch & RV Pick-up; Day 2: Christchurch to Lake Tekapo; Day 3: Lake Tekapo to Aoraki/Mount Cook (including a shorter walk like Hooker Valley Track) & On to Queenstown; Day 4: Queenstown - Adventure & Relaxation; Day 5: Queenstown to Te Anau; Day 6: Milford Sound Day Trip; Day 7: Te Anau to Wanaka; Day 8: Wanaka - Scenic Beauty & Quiet Time; Day 9: Wanaka to Franz Josef/Fox Glacier; Day 10: Franz Josef/Fox Glacier to Punakaiki (Pancake Rocks) (Optional add-on: Franz Josef heli-hike, highly weather-dependent and time-constrained, in the morning); Day 11: Punakaiki to Arthur's Pass & Return to Christchurch; Day 12: Depart Christchurch. Key attractions include Milford Sound, Lake Tekapo (Church of the Good Shepherd, stargazing), Queenstown (Skyline Gondola, jet boat, Lake Wakatipu), Franz Josef/Fox Glacier, Punakaiki (Pancake Rocks), and Aoraki/Mount Cook. The trip also features relaxed time in places like Wanaka and scenic drives through Lindis Pass, Haast Pass, and Arthur's Pass. Stargazing is a primary activity for Lake Tekapo, with a contingency plan: if weather prevents stargazing at Lake Tekapo, the backup activity is Tekapo Hot Springs, and a second attempt at stargazing will be considered the following night at Aoraki/Mount Cook by checking the forecast. The itinerary also integrates local food recommendations and dining options, reinforcing the comprehensive RV meal plan and restaurant suggestions.
